title: Atropis borreri
intro: Atropis borreri, also known as Borrer's yarrow or Borrer's ligularia, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating a range of ailments such as wounds and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Atropis borreri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Atropis borreri include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat minor injuries and infections.
taxonomy: Atropis borreri is classified as a species within the genus Atropis, which is part of the family Apiaceae. This classification places it among the carrot and parsley family. The genus Atropis is a group of flowering plants commonly known as umbrella plants or umbrella parsley. These plants are native to China and Japan and are known for their umbrella-like inflorescences. The species Atropis borreri is an important component of the traditional medicine in China.
